channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — A jury convicted two men of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: MARK VANCLEAVE and MICHAEL GOLDBERG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Matt Gaetz says he won't return to Congress next year after withdrawing name for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former Rep. Matt Gaetz said Friday that he will not be returning to Congress after withdrawing his name from consideration to be attorney general under President-elect Donald Trump amid growing allegations of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Northern California gets record rain and heavy snow. Many have been in the dark for days in Seattle
item.description: FORESTVILLE, Calif. (AP) — A major storm continued to drop heavy snow and record rain Friday as it moved through Northern California, closing roads and prompting evacuations in some areas, after killing two people and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands in the Pacific Northwest.
item.dc:creator: GODOFREDO A. VÁSQUEZ, JANIE HAR and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Removal of Pride tree inside library draws controversy from residents
item.description: A Christmas tree that displayed support for the LGBTQ+ community was recently taken down at a public library.
item.dc:creator: Sam Chimenti, NewsNation

item.title: Video: Bear cub yanked from tree for 'selfie' released back in the wild
item.description: A bear cub, one of two pulled from a tree as a person posed for a photo, has been returned to the wild.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Young Dolph case placed on a 6 year diversion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- The alleged mastermind behind the killing of Memphis rapper Young Dolph and the man charged with accessory after the fact faced a judge Friday morning. Hernandez Govan is facing charges of attempted and first-degree murder plus conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Cases of 'walking pneumonia' are rising across the US: Here's where it's worst
item.description: In October, the CDC wrote that since late spring, the number of infections caused by walking pneumonia has been increasing, especially among young children.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Rousseau, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prolific Mexican cartel leader allegedly faked his own death to live 'life of luxury' in California
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader, who peddled thousands of kilograms of illegal drugs to the U.S., was killed by a business associate for "lying" — or at least that's what the public was told.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: President-elect Trump announces new choice for attorney general and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Murdered Kansas mom suffered 30 stab, cut wounds trying to defend herself: autopsy
item.description: An Oklahoma medical examiner's office has released the full autopsy report for the one of the two Kansas moms found buried in a freezer in a cow pasture earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your paycheck may be larger in 2025, even if you don't get a raise
item.description: While there's plenty of time left in November, you may already have something exciting to look forward to in 2025: a slightly larger paycheck, even without a raise.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let bodies decay plead guilty to 191 counts of corpse abuse
item.description: Colorado funeral home owners accused of letting nearly 200 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and giving grieving families fake ashes have pleaded guilty to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about a Wisconsin man who faked his own death and fled to Eastern Europe
item.description: A sheriff says a Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning this summer and abandoned his wife and three children is in Eastern Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump has promised again to release the last JFK files. But experts say don’t expect big revelations
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — More than 60 years after President John F. Kennedy was assassinated, conspiracy theories still swirl and any new glimpse into the fateful day of Nov. 22, 1963, in Dallas continues to fascinate.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC stabbing spree shows failures of criminal justice system: Mayor
item.description: “It’s a public health safety issue,” Adams said regarding the triple homicide at a news conference Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Dakota Republican introduces bill to eliminate Department of Education
item.description: The proposal would get rid of the federal agency and move all critical programs to other departments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: There are massive Black Friday deals on coveted North Face gear
item.description: Whether you need a warm winter coat for your kids, a snowsuit for anyone in your family or some sleeping bags and shoes — this sale has you covered.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Carte, NewsNation

item.title: Walmart cinnamon rolls, danish recalled over possible plastic contamination
item.description: "A sales restriction was placed on impacted products, and they were removed from impacted stores," the statement said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police warn of sophisticated holiday scam artists targeting consumers
item.description: He said any time shoppers use their credit cards or personal information, they should go in with their eyes open.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: 73% worry Social Security won't pay promised benefits: Survey
item.description: Most nonretired U.S. adults, 73%, are worried they won't get their promised Social Security benefits when they retire.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Police report reveals assault allegations against Hegseth, Trump's pick for defense secretary
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA, BRIAN SLODYSKO and JULIET LINDERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, WFLA, NBC
